sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: ἵππους ἄγεσθαι , τοῖσι δ ’ αὖ τὰ μείζονα
sections.translation: horses for their prize , but those who conquered in severer feats , in boxing and wrestling , won herds of oxen , and this woman was to be added thereto ; with such a chance ’ twere shame indeed to pass so fair a guerdon by .

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ κλοπαίαν , ἀλλὰ σὺν πόνῳ λαβὼν
sections.translation: So thou must take her in thy charge , as I said ; for not by theft but honest toil I won the prize I bring ; and maybe e ’ en thou in time wilt thank me .

sections.label: Section 1037
sections.original: οὔτοι σ ’ ἀτίζων οὐδ ’ ἐν αἰσχροῖσιν τιθεὶς
sections.translation: ’ Twas not because of any slight or unkind thought of thee that I concealed my wife ’ s sad fate ; but this were adding grief to grief

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: εἴ του πρὸς ἄλλου δώμαθ ’ ὡρμήθης ξένου ·
sections.translation: if thou hadst gone from hence to the halls of some other friend ; and it sufficed that I should mourn my sorrow . But I do beseech thee , prince , if ’ tis possible , bid some other Thessalian , one who hath not suffered as I have , keep the maiden for thee ,— and thou hast many

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: ξένοι Φεραίων · μή μ ’ ἀναμνήσῃς κακῶν .
sections.translation: friends in Pherae ; remind me not of my misfortune . For I could not see her in my house and stay my tears . Oh ! add not new affliction to my stricken heart , for sure by sorrow am I bowed enough . And where within my halls could a tender maiden live ?

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: νέα γάρ , ὡς ἐσθῆτι καὶ κόσμῳ πρέπει .
sections.translation: for such she is , as her dress and vesture show . Is she to dwell where men consort ? Then how shall she retain her maiden purity , if ’ mid our youths she come and go ? O Heracles , it is no easy task to check a young man ’ s fancy , and I am anxious for thy sake .

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: ἢ τῆς θανούσης θάλαμον ἐσβήσας τρέφω ;
sections.translation: Or am I to take her to my dead wife ’ s bower and care for her ? How can I bring her there to fill the other ’ s bed ? Twofold reproach I fear ; first , some fellow-townsman may taunt me with betraying my benefactress in eagerness to wed a new young bride ;

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: καὶ τῆς θανούσης · ἀξία δέ μοι σέβειν ·
sections.translation: next , there is my dead wife , whom I should much regard , for she doth merit all my reverence . Thou too , lady , whosoe ’ er thou art , believe me , art the very counterfeit presentment of Alcestis , the picture of her form , ah me ! O take this maiden , I conjure thee , from my sight ;

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: γυναῖκα τήνδε , μή μ ’ ἕλῃς ᾑρημένον .
sections.translation: slay me not already slain . For in her I seem once more to see my wife ; and my heart is darkly troubled , and the fountains of my eyes are loosed . Ah , woe is me ! Now do I taste the bitterness of this my grief .

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: ἐγὼ μὲν οὐκ ἔχοιμ ’ ἂν εὖ λέγειν τύχην ·
sections.translation: Indeed I cannot call thy fortune blest , yet heaven ’ s gift must thou endure , whoe ’ er the god that comes to bring it .

sections.label: Section 1072
sections.original: εἰ γὰρ τοσαύτην δύναμιν εἶχον ὥστε σὴν
sections.translation: Would I had the power to bring thy wife up to the light from the halls of death , and confer this kindness on thee !

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: σάφ ’ οἶδα βούλεσθαί σ ’ ἄν . ἀλλὰ ποῦ τόδε ;
sections.translation: Right well I know thou wouldst . But what of that ? The dead can never come to life again .

sections.label: Section 1077
sections.original: μή νυν ὑπέρβαλλʼ , ἀλλ ’ ἐναισίμως φέρε .
sections.translation: Do not exceed the mark , but bear thy grief with moderation .

sections.label: Section 1078
sections.original: ῥᾷον παραινεῖν ἢ παθόντα καρτερεῖν .
sections.translation: ’ Tis easier to advise than to suffer and endure .

sections.label: Section 1079
sections.original: τί δ ’ ἂν προκόπτοις , εἰ θέλοις ἀεὶ στένειν ;
sections.translation: Yet what thy gain , if thou for aye wilt mourn ?

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: ἔγνωκα καὐτός , ἀλλ ’ ἔρως τις ἐξάγει .
sections.translation: I too know that myself , but some strange yearning leads me on .

sections.label: Section 1081
sections.original: τὸ γὰρ φιλῆσαι τὸν θανόντ ’ ἄγει δάκρυ .
sections.translation: Love for the dead compels a tear .

sections.label: Section 1082
sections.original: ἀπώλεσέν με , κἄτι μᾶλλον ἢ λέγω .
sections.translation: Her death was mine , more than any words of mine can tell .

sections.label: Section 1083
sections.original: γυναικὸς ἐσθλῆς ἤμπλακες · τίς ἀντερεῖ ;
sections.translation: Thou hast lost a noble wife ; who shall gainsay it ?

sections.label: Section 1084
sections.original: ὥστ ’ ἄνδρα τόνδε μηκέθ ’ ἥδεσθαι βίῳ .
sections.translation: Life henceforth hath lost all charm for me .

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: χρόνος μαλάξει , νῦν δ ’ ἔθ ’ ἡβάσκει κακόν .
sections.translation: Time will soothe the smart ; as yet thy grief is young .

sections.label: Section 1086
sections.original: χρόνον λέγοις ἄν , εἰ χρόνος τὸ κατθανεῖν .
sections.translation: Time ! use that word , if death and time are one .

sections.label: Section 1087
sections.original: γυνή σε παύσει καὶ νέου γάμου πόθοι .
sections.translation: A new wife and a longing for a fresh marriage will stay thy sorrow .

sections.label: Section 1088
sections.original: σίγησον · οἷον εἶπας . οὐκ ἂν ᾠόμην .
sections.translation: Peace ! What words are thine ? I ne ’ er of thee had thought it .

sections.label: Section 1089
sections.original: τί δʼ ; οὐ γαμεῖς γάρ , ἀλλὰ χηρεύσῃ λέχος ;
sections.translation: What ! wilt never wed , but preserve thy widowed state ?

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν ἥτις τῷδε συγκλιθήσεται .
sections.translation: There is no woman living that shall share my couch .

sections.label: Section 1091
sections.original: μῶν τὴν θανοῦσαν ὠφελεῖν τι προσδοκᾷς ;
sections.translation: Dost think that this will help the dead at all ?

sections.label: Section 1092
sections.original: κείνην ὅπουπερ ἔστι τιμᾶσθαι χρεών .
sections.translation: My reverence she deserves , where ’ er she is .

sections.label: Section 1093
sections.original: αἰνῶ μὲν αἰνῶ · μωρίαν δ ’ ὀφλισκάνεις .
sections.translation: I praise thee , yes ; but still thou bringest on thyself the charge of folly .

sections.label: Section 1094
sections.original: ὡς μήποτ ’ ἄνδρα τόνδε νυμφίον καλῶν .
sections.translation: So that thou never call ’ st me bridegroom , praise me if thou wilt .

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original: ἐπῄνεσ ’ ἀλόχῳ πιστὸς οὕνεκ ’ εἶ φίλος .
sections.translation: I praise thee for thy loyalty to thy wife .

sections.label: Section 1096
sections.original: θάνοιμ ’ ἐκείνην καίπερ οὐκ οὖσαν προδούς .
sections.translation: Come death ! if ever I betray her , dead though she be .

sections.label: Section 1097
sections.original: δέχου νυν εἴσω τήνδε γενναίων δόμων .
sections.translation: Well , take this maiden to the shelter of thy noble house .

sections.label: Section 1098
sections.original: μή , πρός σε τοῦ σπείραντος ἄντομαι Διός .
sections.translation: Spare me , I entreat thee by Zeus , thy sire .

sections.label: Section 1099
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἁμαρτήσῃ γε μὴ δράσας τάδε .
sections.translation: Be sure , if thou refuse , ’ twill be a sad mistake .

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: καὶ δρῶν γε λύπῃ καρδίαν δηχθήσομαι .
sections.translation: If I comply , remorse will gnaw my heart .

sections.label: Section 1101
sections.original: πιθοῦ · τάχ ’ ἂν γὰρ ἐς δέον πέσοι χάρις .
sections.translation: Yield ; for in god ’ s good time maybe thou wilt give me thanks .

sections.label: Section 1102
sections.original: φεῦ .
sections.translation: Ah ! would thou hadst never won her in the games !

sections.label: Section 1103
sections.original: νικῶντι μέντοι καὶ σὺ συννικᾷς ἐμοί .
sections.translation: Yet thou too sharest in my victory .

sections.label: Section 1104
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξας · ἡ γυνὴ δ ’ ἀπελθέτω .
sections.translation: True ; still let this maiden go away .

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: ἄπεισιν , εἰ χρή · πρῶτα δ ’ εἰ χρεὼν ἄθρει .
sections.translation: Go she shall , if go she must ; but first see if this is needful .

sections.label: Section 1106
sections.original: χρή , σοῦ γε μὴ μέλλοντος ὀργαίνειν ἐμοί .
sections.translation: I needs must , else wilt thou be wroth with me .

sections.label: Section 1107
sections.original: εἰδώς τι κἀγὼ τήνδ ’ ἔχω προθυμίαν .
sections.translation: I have a reason good to press the matter thus .

sections.label: Section 1108
sections.original: νίκα νυν . οὐ μὴν ἁνδάνοντά μοι ποιεῖς .
sections.translation: Have thy way then . Yet know well thy deed I disapprove .

sections.label: Section 1109
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ἔσθ ’ ὅθ ’ ἡμᾶς αἰνέσεις · πιθοῦ μόνον .
sections.translation: A day will come that thou wilt praise me ; only yield .

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: κομίζετʼ , εἰ χρὴ τήνδε δέξασθαι δόμοις .
sections.translation: ( to his servants ). Take her in , if I needs must give her welcome in my house .

sections.label: Section 1111
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν μεθείην τὴν γυναῖκα προσπόλοις .
sections.translation: To thy servants will I not hand her over .

sections.label: Section 1112
sections.original: σὺ δ ’ αὐτὸς αὐτὴν εἴσαγʼ , εἰ βούλῃ , δόμους .
sections.translation: Conduct her then thyself within , if so thou thinkest good .

sections.label: Section 1113
sections.original: ἐς σὰς μὲν οὖν ἔγωγε θήσομαι χέρας .
sections.translation: Nay , but into thy hands shall mine consign her .

sections.label: Section 1114
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν θίγοιμι · δῶμα δ ’ εἰσελθεῖν πάρα .
sections.translation: I will not touch her , though she is free to go within my halls .

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: τῇ σῇ πέποιθα χειρὶ δεξιᾷ μόνῃ .
sections.translation: To thy hand , and thine alone I her entrust .

sections.label: Section 1116
sections.original: ἄναξ , βιάζῃ μ ’ οὐ θέλοντα δρᾶν τάδε .
sections.translation: Prince , against my will thou dost constrain me to this deed .

sections.label: Section 1117
sections.original: τόλμα προτεῖναι χεῖρα καὶ θιγεῖν ξένης .
sections.translation: Boldly stretch out thy hand and touch the stranger maid .

sections.label: Section 1118
sections.original: καὶ δὴ προτείνω , Γοργόν ’ ὡς καρατομῶν .
sections.translation: There , then , I stretch it out as toward the Gorgon ’ s severed head . [*]

sections.label: Section 1119
sections.original: ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: Hast hold of her ?

sections.label: Section 1119b
sections.original: ἔχω ; ναί .
sections.translation: I have .

sections.label: Section 1119c
sections.original: σῷζέ νυν , καὶ τὸν Διὸς
sections.translation: ( removes the veil ). So ; keep her safely then , and

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: φήσεις ποτ ’ εἶναι παῖδα γενναῖον ξένον .
sections.translation: in days to come thou wilt confess the son of Zeus proved himself a noble guest . Look well at her , if haply to thy gaze she have a semblance of thy wife ; and now that thou art blest , cease from sorrowing .

sections.label: Section 1123
sections.original: ὦ θεοί , τί λέξω — θαῦμ ’ ἀνέλπιστον τόδε —
sections.translation: Great gods , what shall I say ? a marvel past all hope is here ! My wife , my own true wife I see ,

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: ἢ κέρτομός με θεοῦ τις ἐκπλήσσει χαρά ;
sections.translation: or is some mocking rapture sent by heaven to drive me mad ?
